zh.lifeSummary: 當John Stone of Great Bromley於1528年1月1日出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛時，他的父親John Arthur Burton of Hadlow23歲，母親Lady Agnes Clarke21歲。他於1553年與Agnes Barton Stone在英國英格蘭薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1597年5月27日在他的家鄉享年69歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Stone of Great Bromley于1528年1月1日出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛时，他的父亲John Arthur Burton of Hadlow23岁，母亲Lady Agnes Clarke21岁。他于1553年与Agnes Barton Stone在英国英格兰薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1597年5月27日在他的家乡享年69岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛。

nameMeanings.name: Stone
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone, rock’ (Old English stān). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on stony ground, by a notable outcrop of rock, or by a stone boundary-marker or monument, or habitational, from a place called Stone, such as those in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Staffordshire, and Worcestershire.
Irish (Kilkenny): adopted for Irish Ó Clochartaigh (see Clougherty ) and/or Ó Clochasaigh (see Clohessy ), and possibly several other names containing or thought to contain the element cloch ‘stone’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of various surnames in other languages, meaning ‘stone’, including Jewish Stein , Norwegian Steine, French Lapierre .

variants.name: Stoner
meanings: English (Sussex): topographic name from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone’ + -er, used for someone who lived by a prominent rock or stone, or perhaps who came from a place called Stone, although this formation is more rare. See Stone and compare Stoneman .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Steiner  .

variants.name: Stones
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of Stone with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. A number of the places called Stone are sometimes recorded with plural spellings in the medieval period, including Stone (Staffordshire), recorded as Stanes in 1187.

variants.name: Stenner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt.

German: occupational name for a stonemason or stonecutter, or a topographic name for someone who lived on stony ground, from a derivative of Middle High German stein ‘stone’, ‘stony place’. Compare Stone and Stein .
English: variant of   Stayner  .

variants.name: Clougherty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Clochartaigh ‘descendant of Clochartach’, a personal name, probably formed with cloch ‘stone’, Stone being an alternative Anglicized form of the surname.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Stoneman
meanings: English: perhaps an occupational name for someone who was the servant (Middle English man) of a person surnamed Stone . Alternatively, the name may have been an alias of Stone , used for someone who lived by a prominent stone or rock or at a minor place so named (Middle English ston + man).
English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name for someone who worked with stone or who sold precious stones; a jeweler.

English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Ston(e)man (Old English Stān(e)mann), a pet form of Old English personal names beginning with Stān- (from the word ‘stone’, ‘rock’) + the Old English hypocoristic suffix -mann.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Great
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Greet  .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Gross  .

variants.name: Greet
meanings: English:
nickname from Middle English gret(e) ‘big, stout’ (Old English grēat ‘great, thick, bulky’).
from the Middle English female personal name Greta, probably short for Margareta (see Margaret ).

nameMeanings.name: Bromley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places called Bromley, in Essex, Hertfordshire, Kent (now in Greater London), Greater London, Greater Manchester, Staffordshire, Shropshire, and elsewhere. Most are named with Old English brōm ‘broom’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’, but Bromley (near Bow) in Greater London is from Old English bræmbel ‘bramble’ + lēah.

variants.name: Brimley
meanings: habitational name, perhaps from Brimley in Devon or Brimbley in Stoke Abbott, Dorset, both named with Old English brōm ‘broom’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
variant of   Bromley  .
English:

variants.name: Brumley
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Bromley  . This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John Stone of Great Bromley was born on 1 January 1528, in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Arthur Burton of Hadlow, was 23 and his mother, Lady Agnes Clarke, was 21. He married Agnes Barton Stone in 1553, in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 27 May 1597, in his hometown, at the age of 69, and was buried in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Stone of Great Bromley est né le 1 janvier 1528, à Barton St David, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Arthur Burton of Hadlow, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Clarke, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Barton Stone en 1553, à Barton St David, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 27 mai 1597, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Barton St David, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Stone of Great Bromley am 1. Januar 1528 in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Arthur Burton of Hadlow 23 und seine Mutter Lady Agnes Clarke 21. Er hat 1553 in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom Agnes Barton Stone geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 27. Mai 1597 im Alter von 69 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Stone of Great Bromley è nato il il 1 gennaio 1528 nel Barton St David, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Arthur Burton of Hadlow, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Lady Agnes Clarke, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Barton Stone nel 1553, nel Barton St David, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 27 maggio 1597, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolto a Barton St David, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Stone of Great Bromleyが1528年1月1日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトバートン スト ダヴィドで生まれた時，父John Arthur Burton of Hadlowは23歳，母Lady Agnes Clarkeは21歳であった。1553年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトバートン スト ダヴィドでAgnes Barton Stoneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1597年5月27日に故郷で69歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトバートン スト ダヴィドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Stone of Great Bromley(이)가 1528년 1월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Arthur Burton of Hadlow(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Agnes Clarke(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1553년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드에서 Agnes Barton Stone(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1597년 5월 27일 그의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Stone of Great Bromley nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1528, em Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Arthur Burton of Hadlow, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Lady Agnes Clarke, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Barton Stone em 1553, em Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 27 de maio de 1597, em sua cidade natal, com 69 anos, e foi sepultado em Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Stone of Great Bromley родился 1 января 1528 г. в местности бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Arthur Burton of Hadlow было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Clarke, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Barton Stone в 1553 г. в местности бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей. Он умер 27 мая 1597 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Stone of Great Bromley nació el 1 de enero de 1528, en Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Arthur Burton of Hadlow, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Lady Agnes Clarke, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Agnes Barton Stone en 1553, en Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 27 de mayo de 1597, en su pueblo, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultado en Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
